meta_title: For Freelancers: user onboarding to Solve no design skills (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no design skills. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: User Onboarding for Non-Designers
content.seo_description: Learn how to build high-converting user onboarding without design skills. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for fast delivery.
content.answer_block: Overcome the 'no design skills' barrier by leveraging AI-driven UI components. Use Antigravity to generate clean, accessible onboarding layouts and Claude Code to automate the implementation. By focusing on modular, component-based onboarding flows, you can ship professional-grade user experiences in hours, not weeks, significantly increasing your client project value.
content.intro:
You’ve built the backend, the API is snappy, and the logic is sound. Then you show the client the UI, and the silence is deafening. Without a background in design, your onboarding screens look like a graveyard of poorly aligned divs and uninspired text. It is the silent killer of freelance careers, turning high-quality code into 'amateur' projects.

But here is the secret: design is just another system to be engineered. By treating UI as a programmatic challenge rather than an artistic one, you can bypass the need for a design degree. Using Antigravity for structural precision and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you will build onboarding flows that convert, impress, and keep your clients coming back for more.

steps.title: Define the User Journey Map
steps.description: Before touching code, map out the 3-5 critical steps a user must take to reach the 'Aha!' moment. Keep it lean to ensure maximum conversion.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a UX strategist. Create a 4-step onboarding flow for a SaaS dashboard. Focus on progressive disclosure: 1. Welcome, 2. Profile setup, 3. Preference selection, 4. Success state. Provide a clean, minimalist structural schema for each step.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --file onboarding_plan.md --content 'Generate a React component structure based on this 4-step user journey.'

steps.title: Generate Structural UI Components
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the layout scaffolding. This prevents 'design paralysis' by giving you a solid, professional foundation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive React component for a user onboarding card. Include a progress bar, a title slot, a content area, and a footer with 'Back' and 'Next' buttons. Use Tailwind CSS for utility-first styling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write --file src/components/OnboardingCard.tsx --prompt 'Implement the Antigravity structure with Tailwind styling.'

steps.title: Automate State Management
steps.description: Onboarding requires tracking progress. We'll use a simple hook-based approach to toggle between steps without bloated libraries.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a custom React hook 'useOnboarding' that manages the current step index, provides a 'next' and 'previous' function, and ensures the index stays within the 0 to 3 range.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write --file src/hooks/useOnboarding.ts --prompt 'Generate the state logic for a multi-step onboarding wizard.'

steps.title: Inject Content and Validation
steps.description: Now, fill the structure with high-intent copy and basic input validation to ensure data integrity during sign-up.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write copy for a 4-step onboarding flow for a task management app. Focus on benefit-driven language. Add Zod schema validation rules for a profile input step.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit src/components/OnboardingCard.tsx --prompt 'Integrate Zod validation and update the card text with the provided copy.'

steps.title: Final Integration and Polish
steps.description: Connect the onboarding flow to your main application state and ensure the transition animations are smooth.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest a simple CSS transition or Framer Motion configuration to animate the sliding effect between onboarding steps.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'npx framer-motion' && claude edit src/components/OnboardingCard.tsx --prompt 'Apply slide animations to the card transitions.'

pro_tips: Always limit onboarding to 3-5 steps; anything longer causes drop-offs.
Use a progress bar to reduce cognitive load by showing the user the end is in sight.
Never ask for payment details during the first onboarding step; build value first.
Use 'Empty States' as part of the onboarding to guide users on how to populate their dashboard.
Leverage the 'Skip' button for power users who want to bypass the tutorial.
content.next_steps: Once the onboarding flow is live, implement a tracking event on the final step to measure conversion rates and iterate based on real user behavior.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting design limitations dictate your project success. With these tools, you can ship world-class onboarding flows that justify your rates and delight your clients. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
